Hero.subheadline: Deploy AI agents that reason about your stack, execute real actions, and destroy themselves when done. Ephemeral pods, permanent results.
Hero.microcopy: Access granted on a first-come, first-served basis based on system capacity.

Urgency.headline: Your team of 4 engineers can't be a team of 40.
Urgency.subheadline: Engineering teams in LatAm operate with limited resources and unlimited responsibilities. Three scenarios you'll recognize.

infrastructure.title: Incident response
infrastructure.narrative: 3:17 AM. Prometheus alert. A pod in CrashLoopBackOff. Your on-call engineer opens the laptop, reviews logs, identifies the problem, scales the deployment. 45 minutes. The same incident, the same solution, the third time this month.
infrastructure.label: What an agent resolves in < 2 min

operations.title: Onboarding and compliance
operations.narrative: Onboarding takes 3 days. It should take 3 hours. Verify documents, cross-check risk lists, generate reports, notify the legal team. Your ops team does copy-paste between 6 tools.
operations.label: Reducible to hours with orchestrated agents

sales.title: Prospecting that doesn't scale
sales.narrative: You have 500 prospects in the CRM. Nobody works them. Your sales team spends 70% of their time on data tasks instead of talking to customers. Enrich, classify, prioritize, send follow-ups. All manual.
sales.stat: Typical: < 20% of sales time dedicated to selling
sales.label: Automatable end to end

HowItWorks.headline: Architecture designed for production from day one.
HowItWorks.subheadline: Each execution follows a deterministic flow with complete isolation. No persistent servers, no shared state, no surprises.

trigger.label: TRIGGER
trigger.description: Cron, Slack, email IMAP, webhook, or manual invocation

channels.label: channels-service
channels.description: Evaluates CEL rules to determine if the event qualifies

redis.label: Redis Stream
redis.description: Durable queue that guarantees delivery and enables replay

k8s.label: K8s Pod
k8s.description: Ephemeral pod with configurable CPU/memory, created on-demand

react.label: ReAct Loop
react.description: Reasoning + Acting with configurable iterations and tool calling

result.label: Result
result.description: Output stored, notifications sent, metrics recorded

cleanup.label: Cleanup
cleanup.description: Pod destroyed. Zero residue, zero idle cost

tooltips.reactLoop: Reasoning + Acting: the agent reasons about context, chooses a tool, observes the result, and decides the next step
tooltips.celRules: Common Expression Language: evaluates conditions on the event before queuing it
tooltips.ephemeralPod: K8s container created for this execution and destroyed when finished. No idle cost.

k8s.title: Automatic pod recovery
k8s.narrative: An agent monitors Prometheus, detects anomalies, and executes corrective actions. Pod restarts, horizontal scaling, deployment rollbacks. All without waking anyone up.

outbound.title: Automated B2B prospecting
outbound.narrative: An agent enriches prospects from the CRM, classifies by ICP, generates personalized messages, and schedules follow-ups. The sales team only intervenes when there's real interest.

gitops.title: Intelligent GitOps reconciliation
gitops.narrative: An agent compares the desired state in Git against the real cluster state. Detects drift, generates the diff, and can apply the correction or request human approval before acting.

emptyStates.noAgents: You don't have any agents configured yet. Create your first one in less than 5 minutes.
emptyStates.noExecutions: This agent hasn't executed yet. Launch a manual execution or configure a trigger.

UseCases.headline: Three problems engineering teams solve with Passflow.
UseCases.subheadline: Not demos. These are production patterns that teams of 4-20 engineers already implement.

k8sOps.title: Autonomous Kubernetes operations
k8sOps.summary: Agents that monitor, diagnose, and repair your cluster without human intervention. From alert to resolution in less than 2 minutes.
k8sOps.detail: Connect Prometheus Alertmanager as trigger. The agent receives the alert, queries cluster state via kubectl, analyzes logs from the affected pod, and executes the corrective action: restart, scale, or rollback. If the problem requires human intervention, it escalates to Slack with the complete diagnosis.
k8sOps.result: 60-80% reduction in response time to repetitive incidents

outboundB2B.title: Intelligent B2B prospecting
outboundB2B.summary: Automatic enrichment, ICP classification, and personalized sequences. Your sales team only talks to qualified prospects.
outboundB2B.detail: The agent takes new prospects from the CRM, enriches data with external APIs (LinkedIn, Clearbit, public data), classifies according to your Ideal Customer Profile using a local LLM via Ollama, generates personalized messages by vertical, and schedules the follow-up sequence. When a prospect responds, it notifies the assigned salesperson.
outboundB2B.result: 3-5x more prospects contacted per salesperson per week

gitops.title: GitOps with intelligent reconciliation
gitops.summary: Detects drift between Git and your cluster, generates readable diffs, and applies corrections with optional human approval.
gitops.detail: Every 15 minutes, an agent compares the manifests in your repository against the current cluster state. If it detects drift, it generates a report with the exact diff, classifies severity, and decides whether to apply automatically (low risk) or request approval via Slack (high risk). Maintains a complete record of each reconciliation.
gitops.result: 90% reduction in undetected drift between environments

Differentiation.headline: What the others don't tell you they don't have.
Differentiation.subheadline: Most automation tools were designed for no-code. Passflow was designed for engineering teams that need control, isolation, and observability.

isolation.passflow: Ephemeral K8s pods with dedicated resources

llmRouting.passflow: 6 providers + local Ollama classification + circuit breaker

reactLoop.tooltip: Reasoning + Acting: the agent reasons, chooses a tool, observes the result, and decides the next step. Configurable iterations.

humanInTheLoop.tooltip: Approval system that pauses execution until a human authorizes the action via Slack, email, or dashboard.
humanInTheLoop.passflow: Native approval system with configurable timeout

mcpNative.tooltip: Model Context Protocol: Anthropic's open standard for LLMs to interact with external tools.

eventDriven.tooltip: Common Expression Language: language for defining conditions on events before executing an agent.
eventDriven.passflow: Redis Streams + CEL + cron + IMAP + webhooks

multiTenancy.passflow: Workspaces with AES credentials + granular RBAC

coldStart.tooltip: Time from receiving the trigger until the agent pod is executing code.

Differentiation.footnote: Data based on public documentation from each platform as of April 2026.

circuitBreaker.definition: If an LLM provider fails, Passflow automatically redirects to the next one without losing the execution

cel.definition: Common Expression Language: filters events with logical expressions before executing the agent

rbac.definition: Controls who can view, edit, and execute each agent and workspace

Trust.headline: Built to pass audits, not to dodge them.
Trust.subheadline: Each component was designed with Latin American financial regulation in mind. SOC 2 readiness, data under your control, and complete traceability.

dataResidency.title: Data residency
dataResidency.description: Choose where your data lives. Deploy on OKE (Oracle Cloud Mexico), AWS LatAm, or your own infrastructure. Data never leaves the region you configure.
dataResidency.technical: Compatible with OKE, EKS, GKE, and on-premise clusters. Each workspace can have its own region configuration.

encryptedCredentials.title: Encrypted credentials
encryptedCredentials.description: Each workspace stores its credentials with AES-256 and unique keys per tenant. Not even the Passflow team can access your secrets.
encryptedCredentials.technical: Encryption at rest with AES-256-GCM. Keys derived per workspace. Programmable key rotation. TLS 1.3 in transit.

auditTrail.title: Complete traceability
auditTrail.description: Every execution, every agent decision, every tool call is recorded. Exportable, queryable, and ready for audits.
auditTrail.technical: Structured logs with execution context, ReAct loop decision, inputs/outputs from each tool, and precise timestamps. Configurable retention.

rbacApprovals.title: Access control and approvals
rbacApprovals.description: Granular roles per workspace and agent. Native approval system for high-risk actions. No one executes what they shouldn't.
rbacApprovals.technical: RBAC with customizable roles (viewer, editor, operator, admin). Human-in-the-loop with approval via Slack, email, or dashboard. Configurable timeout with fallback.

OpenSource.headline: Open core. Cloud power.
OpenSource.subheadline: The agent engine is open source and free forever. Deploy on your infrastructure or let us handle it.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: Self-host the complete agent engine on your infrastructure. Full control, zero vendor lock-in.

OpenSource.note: The open source version includes everything you need to run production agents. Cloud adds convenience and enterprise features.

community.ctaSubtext: Deploy on your K8s, Docker, or bare metal.

faq.exceedTokens: Executions pause until the next cycle. No surprise charges. You can upgrade anytime.
faq.downgrade: Yes, anytime. The change applies in your next billing cycle.
faq.enterprise: Yes. Contact sales for custom volume, dedicated SLAs, and on-premise deployment.

FinalCTA.headline: Your first agent in production in less than an hour.
FinalCTA.subheadline: Three steps. No sales calls, no contracts, no weeks of configuration.

connect.title: Connect your tools
connect.description: Add your service credentials: Kubernetes, CRM, Slack, GitHub. All encrypted with AES-256 from minute one.

Tooltips.reactLoop: Reasoning + Acting: the agent reasons, chooses a tool, observes the result, and decides the next step. Configurable iterations.
Tooltips.mcp: Model Context Protocol: Anthropic's open standard for LLMs to interact with external tools.
Tooltips.ephemeralPod: Kubernetes container created exclusively for this execution. Destroyed when finished. Zero idle cost.
Tooltips.circuitBreaker: Resilience pattern: if an LLM provider fails, the system automatically redirects to the next one without losing the execution.
Tooltips.cel: Common Expression Language: language for defining conditions on events before executing an agent.
Tooltips.byok: Bring Your Own Key: use your own LLM provider API keys for greater control and savings.
Tooltips.rbac: Role-Based Access Control: defines who can view, edit, and execute each resource.
Tooltips.redisStreams: Durable message queue that guarantees delivery, enables replay, and decouples triggers from executions.
Tooltips.humanInTheLoop: Approval system that pauses execution until a human authorizes the action via Slack, email, or dashboard.
Tooltips.coldStart: Time from receiving the trigger until the agent pod is executing code.

CLI.subheadline: Define, validate, and deploy AI agents using PAL (Passflow Agent Language). GitOps-ready from day one.
CLI.platforms: macOS, Linux, Windows • Single binary • No dependencies

features.title: Built for developers who prefer the command line

fast.title: Lightning fast
fast.description: Single Go binary. No runtime dependencies. Instant startup.

secure.title: Secure by default
secure.description: Credentials stored in ~/.passflow with restricted permissions.

portable.title: Fully portable
portable.description: Same commands work on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

gitops.title: GitOps ready
gitops.description: Agent configs in YAML. Version control, review, and CI/CD integration.

commands.title: Powerful commands, simple syntax
commands.subtitle: Everything you need to manage agents from PAL files. Validate before deploy, diff before apply.

validate.description: Validate PAL file syntax and semantics before applying.

apply.description: Create or update an agent from a PAL file. Supports dry-run.

export.description: Export an existing agent configuration as PAL (YAML or JSON).

diff.description: Compare an agent's current config with a local PAL file.

list.description: List all agents in a workspace with status and metadata.

get.description: Get detailed information about a specific agent.

pal.title: PAL: Passflow Agent Language
pal.description: Declarative YAML format for defining AI agents. Human-readable, machine-parseable, version-controllable.

features.declarative: Declarative configuration — describe what, not how
features.versionable: Version controllable — track changes in Git
features.portable: Portable — same format for all environments

cta.title: Start managing agents from your terminal
cta.description: Install the CLI in seconds and deploy your first agent in minutes.

introduction.title: Passflow Documentation
introduction.description: Passflow is an AI agent orchestration platform. Create autonomous agents that reason, use tools, and execute complex tasks on your infrastructure.

react.title: ReAct Loop
react.description: Agents that reason and act in iterative cycles until the task is complete.

isolation.title: Complete Isolation
isolation.description: Each execution runs in its own K8s pod with dedicated resources.

multiProvider.title: Multi-provider LLM
multiProvider.description: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Bedrock, Azure with automatic fallback.

triggers.title: Event-driven
triggers.description: Trigger agents via cron, Slack, email, webhooks, or manually.

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.description: Deploy your first agent in less than an hour.

step1.title: Request access
step1.description: Request access to the platform. Access granted first-come, first-served.

step2.title: Create a workspace
step2.description: Workspaces isolate your agents, credentials, and executions.

step3.title: Configure your first agent
step3.description: Define the persona, model, and available tools.

step4.title: Execute and observe
step4.description: Launch your agent and observe every decision in real time.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Agents are autonomous entities that execute tasks using the ReAct pattern (Reasoning + Acting). Each agent has a persona, instructions, and access to tools.

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.description: Workflows chain multiple agents and actions into complex flows with conditions, approvals, and parallel execution.

agent.title: Agent
agent.description: Executes an agent with its full configuration.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: Evaluates an expression and branches the flow.

action.title: Action
action.description: Executes a simple action (HTTP, notification, etc).

approval.title: Approval
approval.description: Pauses the workflow until human approval.

triggers.title: Triggers
triggers.description: Triggers automatically fire agents when events occur. We support cron, Slack, email IMAP, and webhooks.

cron.title: Cron
cron.description: Execute agents on scheduled times using standard cron expressions.

slack.title: Slack
slack.description: Trigger agents when messages are received in channels or specific mentions.

email.title: Email (IMAP)
email.description: Process incoming emails and trigger agents based on sender, subject, or content.

webhook.title: Webhook
webhook.description: Receive HTTP events from external systems and trigger agents.

cel.title: CEL Filtering
cel.description: Use Common Expression Language to filter events before triggering the agent.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Agents can use tools to interact with external systems. Passflow includes native tools and supports MCP for extensibility.

mcp.title: MCP Tools
mcp.description: Extend your agents' capabilities by connecting custom MCP servers.

api.title: API Reference
api.description: Interact with Passflow programmatically using our REST API.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description: All requests require an API key and workspace ID.

endpoints.title: Main endpoints
endpoints.listAgents: List all agents in the workspace

security.title: Security
security.description: Passflow was designed with enterprise security from the start.

encryption.title: AES-256 Encryption
encryption.description: All credentials are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM and unique keys per workspace.

isolation.title: Per-execution isolation
isolation.description: Each agent runs in an ephemeral K8s pod with dedicated resources and network isolation.

audit.title: Complete audit trail
audit.description: Every action, agent decision, and tool call is recorded with precise timestamps.

compliance.title: SOC 2 Ready
compliance.description: Controls designed to comply with SOC 2 Type II and LATAM financial regulations.

help.title: Need help?
help.description: Our team is available to help you configure your first agents.
